name: Private Olek Szymanski
publicFace: A Polish-American from Chicago who enlisted at seventeen and has the wide, still eyes of someone who has seen something he cannot process.
privateGoal: To tell someone what he saw before he loses consciousness — he does not want the information to die with him.
fear: That no one will stop long enough to listen.
knowledge: He crawled within forty yards of WN-71 before being hit and observed the exact position of the Tobruk machine-gun pit — its concrete lip, its traverse arc, its blind spot.
He saw two German soldiers leave the WN-71 position and move toward the draw's interior at approximately 0645 — suggesting the position may be undermanned.
He knows the grid reference for WN-71 from the pre-assault map briefing and can recite it from memory.
voice: Slow, careful, slightly formal — English is his second language and he chooses words deliberately even when fading.
trustLogic: Responds to anyone who kneels down and looks at him directly — he needs to be treated as a person, not a problem.
secrets: He is not as badly wounded as the medic's initial assessment suggests — the blood is from a scalp laceration, not an internal injury, and he could potentially move under his own power.
He has a photograph in his breast pocket of a woman in Waukegan who does not know he enlisted.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Answers slowly and completely — he has been waiting for someone to ask.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Becomes distressed; his English deteriorates under stress.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not be rushed — pushing him causes him to lose the thread of what he knows.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Cooperative; asks to be helped to the shingle.
